_note: Recorded flagship session: upstream rename customer_id -> cust_id. Drives the demo and Seam-1 rename test deterministically without an LLM key. Tool RESULTS are produced by really running read_file/get_schema/edit_file/run_sandbox. The fix aliases cust_id back to customer_id so the model's OUTPUT contract is preserved (downstream keeps working; output schema unchanged).

turns.text: The model failed referencing a column that no longer exists upstream. Let me read the failing model.
It selects customer_id from raw_customers. Let me check the current upstream columns to confirm the drift.
Confirmed: raw_customers no longer has customer_id; it now exposes cust_id (same type). This is a clean 1:1 rename. I'll alias cust_id back to customer_id so the model's output contract is unchanged.
Now verify the candidate fix in the sandbox before proposing it.

Upstream renamed the source column customer_id to cust_id, which broke the orders model's SELECT. I aliased cust_id back to customer_id so the model compiles, builds on the sample, and keeps its output schema unchanged. The change is a single-column rename on the failing model only; no other files touched.
